title: The AI Quick-Start Pack

intro_callout.title: Why this is free

chapters.title: The 5-Part Prompt Formula
chapters.sub: Why most prompts fail, and the structure that fixes it.

blocks.text: Most people type prompts like a Google search: a few keywords and hope. The model fills the gaps with the most generic answer it can, and you get mediocre output. The fix is to treat every prompt as a short job brief. Give the model a role, the context it cannot guess, the exact format you want back, the constraints that matter, and one worked example. Do that and the same model produces dramatically better work, no special tools required.
The five parts

items: Role: who the model should act as (a senior copywriter, a recruiter, a financial analyst)
Context: the facts it cannot guess (audience, product, goal, prior attempts)
Task: the single, specific thing you want done
Format: how the answer should be shaped (table, 5 bullets, 200 words, JSON)
Constraints: tone, what to avoid, length, reading level

blocks.title: Rule of thumb
blocks.body: If a smart freelancer could not do the task from your prompt alone, the model cannot either. Add the missing brief.

blocks.label: The formula as a template
blocks.text:
You are a [ROLE].
Context: [WHAT THE MODEL CANNOT GUESS].
Task: [THE ONE SPECIFIC THING].
Format: [EXACT SHAPE OF THE ANSWER].
Constraints: [TONE, LENGTH, WHAT TO AVOID].

chapters.title: 12 Prompts You Can Paste Today
chapters.sub: Built with the formula above. Swap the brackets for your details.

blocks.text: These cover the three things people use AI for most: getting work out the door, writing things that persuade, and thinking more clearly. Each one already has a role, a format, and constraints baked in, so you only have to add your specifics.
Work and email

blocks.label: 1 — Inbox zero reply
blocks.text: You are my executive assistant. Draft a reply to the email below. Match the sender's tone, keep it under 90 words, end with one clear next step. Email: [PASTE]

blocks.label: 2 — Meeting to action items
blocks.text: You are a project manager. Turn these messy notes into a table of owner, action, and due date. Flag anything ambiguous. Notes: [PASTE]

blocks.label: 3 — Say no gracefully
blocks.text: You are a diplomatic communicator. Write a 3-sentence decline to [REQUEST] that keeps the relationship warm and offers one alternative.
Writing and marketing

blocks.label: 4 — 10 headlines
blocks.text: You are a direct-response copywriter. Give 10 headlines for [PRODUCT] aimed at [AUDIENCE]. Mix curiosity, benefit, and specificity. No clickbait, no exclamation marks.

blocks.label: 5 — Rewrite for clarity
blocks.text: Rewrite the text below at an 8th-grade reading level. Keep every fact, cut filler, use short sentences. Return only the rewrite. Text: [PASTE]

blocks.label: 6 — Cold outreach
blocks.text: You are a sales rep known for short, human messages. Write a 60-word outreach to [PERSON/ROLE] about [VALUE]. One specific compliment, one clear ask, no buzzwords.
Thinking and planning

blocks.label: 7 — Decision in 5 minutes
blocks.text: Act as a decision coach. I'm choosing between [A] and [B]. Ask me 5 sharp questions one at a time, then recommend one with your reasoning.

blocks.label: 8 — Break down a big goal
blocks.text: You are a planner. Turn [GOAL] into a 7-day plan with one concrete task per day I can finish in under 30 minutes.

blocks.label: 9 — Find the risk
blocks.text: Be a skeptical reviewer. Here is my plan: [PASTE]. List the 3 most likely ways it fails and the cheapest test for each.

blocks.label: 10 — Explain it simply
blocks.text: Explain [TOPIC] to me like I'm smart but new to it. Use one analogy, then 3 bullet takeaways.

blocks.label: 11 — Summarize a long doc
blocks.text: Summarize the text below into: a 1-line gist, 5 key points, and any action I should take. Text: [PASTE]

blocks.label: 12 — Weekly review
blocks.text: Act as my coach. Ask me 4 questions about last week (wins, misses, lessons, next focus), one at a time, then summarize into a short plan.

chapters.title: Your One-Page AI Workflow
chapters.sub: How to actually build the habit so this pays off every day.

blocks.text: Prompts are only useful if you reach for them. The trick is to remove friction: keep your best prompts one keystroke away and batch similar tasks so you stay in the same headspace. Here is the simplest workflow that sticks.

items: Save your 3 most-used prompts somewhere you can paste in one click (a note, a text expander, a pinned doc)
Batch like with like: answer all email in one AI session, write all copy in another
Always give context once at the top of a session, then keep asking follow-ups
Keep a running file of prompts that worked so your library compounds

blocks.title: The 10-minute test
blocks.body: Pick one prompt above, use it on a real task right now, and notice how much time it saved. That number is why the full kits are worth it.

rows: Replies and action items in minutes
A decision, a plan, the risks
